data.title: A yen for carry trades that never left the markets is vexing everyone
data.description:
Yen carry trade is back to dictating the markets.

Highlights:

  • BIS estimates that yen carry trade size was $250 billion as of August

  • Carry trade is a process where yen borrowings are channelled to other currency assets to earn a higher return

  • Carry trade unwinding led to a sharp decline in global equity markets in August and the yen’s appreciation

  • The yen has risen again on expectations of rate hike by Bank of Japan

  • Carry trades are being built again but investors worry an unwind is around the corner

  • US President-elect Donald
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: KKR in final talks to acquire controlling stake in HCG; negotiations on over founder’s role
data.description:
KKR is looking to acquire CVC’s 60.36 percent stake in HCG at Rs 425-450 per share.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

KKR is in advanced negotiations to acquire a controlling stake in Healthcare Global Enterprises Ltd (HCG), India’s largest cancer care hospital chain, from private equity firm CVC Capital Partners, reported The Economic Times, citing unidentified sources. Moneycontrol was the first to report KKR’s exclusivity pact with HCG in October, as the firm emerged as the frontrunner, beating rival bidder Bain Capital.

The deal, which could be finalised by January, involves KKR acquiring CVC’s 60.36 percent stake at Rs 425-450 per share, a 10-15 percent discount to HCG’s closing price of Rs 510 on Tuesday. This translates to a stake valued between Rs 4,663 crore and Rs 4,935 crore, compared to CVC’s initial acquisition cost of Rs 1,049 crore in 2020.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

KKR’s acquisition bid will trigger a mandatory open offer for an additional 26 percent of HCG’s shares from public shareholders. The deal is likely to result in KKR becoming the majority owner, with the possibility of delisting HCG if its total stake exceeds 90 percent after the open offer, reported ET.

Founder’s role at centre of discussions

The negotiations are on over the role of founder Dr BS Ajaikumar at the company post stake sale. KKR’s bid is subject to gaining control, which could require Ajaikumar to step down from the Executive Chairman position.

Also read | Policybazaar parent PB Fintech forays into healthcare services

Ajaikumar, who leads the founding family’s 10.87 percent stake, has expressed reluctance to sell his shares or relinquish his leadership role. “I made it clear, I am not selling,” ET cited Ajaikumar as saying.

HCG’s share price

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

HCG’s share price has gained over 49 percent over the last six months and over 14 percent in the past month alone, taking its market capitalisation to Rs 7,100 crore. For Q2 FY25, the hospital chain reported a 33 percent on-year jump in net profit to Rs 33 crore. Its quarterly revenue was the best ever at Rs 553.5 crore, up 14 percent from a year ago. EBITDA grew 21 percent to Rs 102.3 crore.

HCG operates 22 cancer centres across India and Africa, with 2,200 beds and seven fertility centres under the Milann brand. It recently acquired a 51 percent stake in Visakhapatnam-based Mahatma Gandhi Cancer Hospital & Research Institute for Rs 414 crore.

data.title: Short Call | A little bit of Bitcoin for extra flavour; Aster DM, RBL Bank in focus
data.description:
Short Call

-
  • -
  • -
  • Volume: -
Open Trading A/c
-
0 (0%)
Todays L/H
0
0

While the equity market takes a leisurely stroll toward modest near-term returns, Bitcoin just sprinted through one of its best months this year, leaping nearly 40 percent in November. The ambassador behind this crypto rally? None other than the second coming of Donald Trump as US President, determined to set the crypto world free from its regulatory shackles.

Here’s the kicker: Trump has a plan to turn the 208,109 Bitcoins chilling in federal custody into a strategic national Bitcoin stockpile. That’s over 1 percent of the world’s Bitcoin supply, currently valued at a jaw-dropping $20 billion. On the campaign trail, he’s been pitching himself as the crypto industry’s saviour, promising to bring it out of the shadows of unclear regulations and the SEC’s enforcement-heavy antics under Chair Gary Gensler.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Crypto pundits are now popping champagne bottles, predicting Bitcoin will soon hit the $100,000 milestone. Riding these tailwinds, Jefferies' Christopher Wood’s global portfolio is easing up with a 10 percent allocation to Bitcoin ETFs. His advice? Not to try and trade these positions, most particularly in the pension fund portfolio. Still, for those with a more tactical focus, Wood suggests cashing in some Bitcoins around the $150,000 mark since his base case is that the crypto will rally three times in this post-halving cycle since the trend has been for the capital gains on holding it to more than halve following each halving cycle. A halving cycle is where prices for Bitcoin correct to around half of its recent highs over a period of time following which it sees a retracement towards higher levels.

Bitcoin’s halvings have a history of fueling massive gains. After the first halving in November 2012, Bitcoin skyrocketed 90x in 12 months. The second halving in July 2016 saw a 30x jump in 18 months. The third halving in May 2020 brought a 7.5x rise in 11 months, eventually hitting a record $68,992 in November 2021, marking an 8x gain in 18 months. Since the most recent halving  in April 2023, Bitcoin has already climbed 54 percent to a high of $98,450.

With bullish trends in Bitcoin are now making it hard for investors to ignore it, Trump’s mainstreaming push is giving the crypto an undeniable spotlight. But hold your golden horses—Wood warns against swapping gold for Bitcoin just yet. Instead, think of it as your digital alternative worth a shot. One that is worth giving a try.

RBL Bank (Rs 162.71, +4.3%)

Shares surged over 4 percent, amid heavy volumes. The stock also recovered from its previous day losses after it ended its partnership with Bajaj Finance on cobranded credit cards.

Bull Case: The lender said the move to stop the partnership is in line with its vision to build a diverse range of collaborations with NBFCs such as Mahindra & Mahindra Finance Limited, TVS Finance Limited as well as consumer brands like IOC and IRCTC. The bank’s growth is likely to revive in H2FY25, as the bank scales up arrangements with new co-branding partners.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Bear case: As a result of the move, the bank's headline credit growth could moderate 200 basis points to 13-14 percent in FY25, said Investec. There could be a corresponding decline in NIIs, leading to an estimates downgrade.

Aster DM Healthcare (Rs 483.10; -2.5%)

Shares fell after the company's merger with Blackstone-backed Quality Care India (QCIL) through a share swap agreement.

Bull Case: Combined entity will operate a network of 38 hospitals with over 10,150 beds across 27 cities, positioning it as one of India's top-three hospital chains. The merger strengthens the company's presence in Central and South India, potentially boosting patient volumes and fending off competition.

Bear Case: Elara Securities highlighted that while the deal is fair, it does not foresee immediate value creation for shareholders. Centrum Broking maintained its estimates as the deal is yet to be finalised.

(With inputs from Zoya and Neeshita)
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: World Street | Kospi sinks 2%, Microsoft's faces $1.3 bn lawsuit, UK inflation spikes and more
data.description:
World Street offers a sneak peek into the world of business and economy.

South Korea's benchmark Kospi index dragged 2 percent amid political upheaval in the country.  Microsoft has been charged with a $1.3 billion lawsuit alleging unfairly overcharging customers of rival cloud companies. UK inflation picked up sharply to a higher-than-expected 2.3 percent in October, squashing hopes of a December interest rate cut from the Bank of England. All this and more in the December 4 edition of World Street.

Total chaos

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

South Korea’s markets opened sharply lower Wednesday following a day of political chaos, where President Yoon Suk Yeol imposed and later rescinded a martial law decree.

The Kospi fell 1.8 percent, and the Kosdaq dropped 2.1 percent, slightly paring losses after reports that the financial regulator could deploy a 10 trillion won ($7.07 billion) stabilisation fund to support markets, according to Yonhap News Agency.

Heavy penalty

Microsoft has been hit with a £1 billion ($1.27 billion) lawsuit alleging it unfairly overcharged customers using rival cloud platforms, including Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Alibaba Cloud.

The lawsuit claims Microsoft’s pricing structure forces higher costs on customers licensing its Windows Server software on competitors' infrastructure while offering discounts for its Azure cloud users. Plaintiffs argue this creates an unfair disadvantage, effectively penalizing firms opting for alternative cloud solutions.

Getting out of hand

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Inflation in the UK surged to 2.3 percent in October, up from 1.7 percent in September and surpassing the 2.2 percent forecast, according to the Office for National Statistics. The rise pushes inflation above the Bank of England's 2 percent target, likely curbing chances of a December rate cut.

Pressure mounts

Intel stock tumbled 6 percent overnight, marking its worst day since September, following the abrupt exit of CEO Pat Gelsinger. Gelsinger's four-year tenure saw Intel lose market share and falter in artificial intelligence.

The company announced CFO David Zinsner and products chief MJ Holthaus as interim co-CEOs, with board member Frank Yeary stepping in as interim executive chair. Intel's stock is down over 50 percent this year as the search for a permanent CEO begins.

Shopping spree

Nearly 197 million Americans shopped during Thanksgiving through Cyber Monday, surpassing the National Retail Federation’s forecast of 183.4 million but falling short of 2023's 200 million.

Shoppers spent an average of $235, $8 more than last year, with top purchases including apparel, toys, and personal care items. The data highlights retailers’ success in attracting deal-hungry consumers despite a shorter holiday shopping window.

data.title: Kaynes Tech acquires Austria's Sensonic to ramp up railway infrastructure using AI
data.description:
Ramesh Kunhikannan, Managing Director of Kaynes Technology said the acquisition will advance the company's plans to deliver railway safety and efficiency on a global scale.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Kaynes Tech has acquired an Austria-based company Sensonic, in an effort to enhance its capabilities in railway safety and infrastructure management, the company said on December 3.

The acquisition of a controlling stake of 54% has been executed through a wholly-owned subsidiary Kaynes Holding, and it expands Kaynes' presence in global infrastructure technology markets, said the company. The partnership integrates Sensonic’s fiber optic sensing technologies with Kaynes' track record in manufacturing and IoT innovation.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Ramesh Kunhikannan, Managing Director of Kaynes Technology said the acquisition will advance the company's plans to deliver railway safety and efficiency on a global scale.

Sensonic provides what is known as Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS), a solution to monitor railway infrastructure that combines fiber optic sensing with Artificial Intelligence and machine learning. The Austrian company's services turn optical fibers into vibration sensors, which in turn gather critical infrastructure information along their length.

"A multitude of insights are revealed by listening to the railway, including monitoring of track condition, alerting of landslides and rockfalls, identifying security threats and more," the Kaynes statement said about the acquired company.

Kaynes Tech has a market capitalisation of close to Rs 40,000 crore, and the shares are higher by 135% so far this year.

Recently, Kaynes Technology had acquired smart meter company Iskraemeco India for Rs 43 crore, to boost products and services in energy management.

The company is betting of government's plans on the infrastructure front, and says it is hopeful of more traction on railways in the second half. Kaynes sees a potential business of Rs 15,000-20,000 crore from the Kavach System, as an addressable market and has been working to seek approvals.

Volume

More

Rail Vikas Nigam (RVNL) share price will remain in focus on December 4 after the company received a project worth Rs 186.76 crore from East Central Railway.

".... has received Letter of Acceptance (LoA) from East Central Railway for design, supply, erection, testing & commissioning of traction sub stations with associated switching posts for upgradation work of electric traction system from 1X25 to 2 X 25 KV AT feeding system in Gomoh - Patratu Section of Dhanbad Division of East Central Railway,” company said in its release.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The said project is to be completed in 540 days.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

In the month of November, the company emerged as the lowest bidder (L1) from PSPCL for development of distribution infrastructure work for Package-3 Central Zone for execution of HT/LT Infrastructure loss reduction works under reforms-based and results-linked, revamped distribution sector scheme (RDSS) in the state of Punjab. The cost of the work is Rs 642.56 crore and to be completed in 24 months.

Also, secured a letter of acceptance (LoA) from Eastern Railway for an infrastructure project worth Rs 837.67 crore. The project involves earthwork, bridge construction, and railway track laying across 55.2 kilometers between Kalipahari and Pradhankhuta.

The company had reported 27 percent drop in its net profit at Rs 287 crore in Q2FY25. Its revenue registered a marginal decline, reaching Rs 4,855 crore.

Over the past 12 months, the stock jumped more than 150 percent, while Nifty rose 20 percent during the same period

data.description:
Market Today

Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty 50 are likely to see a flat to negative start on December 4, tracking cues from GIFT Nifty trading around 24,508.50, a short while ago this morning.

Track the latest updates on GIFT Nifty right here on Moneycontrol.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Bulls further tighten their holding on Dalal Steet as the benchmarks extended the gains on third consecutive session on December 3 with Nifty above 24,450, amid buying across sectors, barring FMCG.

At close, the Sensex was up 597.67 points or 0.74 percent at 80,845.75, and the Nifty was up 181.10 points or 0.75 percent at 24,457.15.

Here is how financial markets across the globe fared overnight:
